subject: Re: Warp Missing GPUAcceleration.dll
body:
Hi Abhiram,

while past Windows updates didn't seem to break anything Warp-related, I 
wouldn't rule them out as the cause. I'll release 1.0.6 next week. It's 
compiled with CUDA 10 and the latest versions of all frameworks, so maybe 
it will fix your problem. Meanwhile, re-installing the MSVC++ 
redistributable might help.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Saturday, October 13, 2018 at 1:49:28 AM UTC+2, achintangal@berkeley.edu 
wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
>
> I setup Warp on a new workstation with two 1080ti's. I was able to test it 
> with a small data-set. 
> Today, it complains that it cannot find the GPUAcceleration.dll 
>
> The system has the following installed. 
>
>  - Nvidia Driver 416.16
> -  Cuda 10.0.132
> -  .NET framework 4.7.2
>
> Any ideas? I  had to restart the system today to add a new disk. Windows 
> installed some updates and that's pretty much it. 
>
> Reinstalling Warp didn't work too.
>
> Thanks for your time,
>
> Abhiram
>
